executive_summary.one_paragraph: VIM (vimentin) displays confirmed cell-surface accessibility in multiple independent human cell contexts: cancer cell lines (live-cell flow cytometry, antibody 7C11-D9), Huh-7.5.1 hepatocellular carcinoma cells (surface biotinylation/WB), primary apoptotic neutrophils (live-cell flow), and senescent chondrocytes (cell-based ELISA on intact cells). Surface exposure is strongly state-dependent — preferentially upregulated in cancer, apoptosis, and senescence — consistent with VIM's canonical role as a cytoskeletal intermediate filament whose surface form (CSV) is an unconventional, non-transmembrane presentation.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: VIM surface accessibility is supported by at least two distinct direct method types from independent sources: (1) live-cell flow cytometry without permeabilization using anti-vimentin mAb 7C11-D9 across multiple cancer cell lines (PMC10073919) and on primary human apoptotic neutrophils (PMID:41365250); (2) surface biotinylation (sulfo-NHS) followed by streptavidin pulldown and anti-vimentin Western blot in Huh-7.5.1 hepatocellular carcinoma cells (PMC11744697); and (3) cell-based ELISA on intact non-permeabilized senescent chondrocytes (PMID:41865378). These come from multiple independent groups and cover cancer cell lines, primary immune cells, and primary chondrocytes. No contradicting evidence (intracellular-only or secreted-only) is present in the ledger. The grade is confidently direct_multi_method.

antibodies.name: anti-vimentin monoclonal antibody 7C11-D9
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Validated by immunoassay with cross-species sequence alignment of the vimentin peptide target; no explicit KO control mentioned.

expression_observations.context: Multiple cancer cell lines stained live (non-permeabilized) with anti-vimentin 7C11-D9; cell-surface vimentin (CSV) detected across the panel

antibodies.name: anti-vimentin antibody (Western blot readout)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Used as WB detection reagent after sulfo-NHS biotinylation and streptavidin pulldown; no clone or vendor specified in source.

expression_observations.context: Sulfo-NHS cell surface biotinylation of intact Huh-7.5.1 hepatocellular carcinoma cells, streptavidin pulldown, anti-vimentin WB confirms cell-surface vimentin (CSV) present

antibodies.name: anti-vimentin antibody (surface staining, apoptotic neutrophils)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: No clone, vendor, or validation strategy specified; used to detect Vim and citrullinated Vim (cit-Vim) at cell surface of apoptotic neutrophils.

expression_observations.context: Vimentin (Vim) and citrullinated vimentin (cit-Vim) detected at cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils; caspase-mediated degradation implicated in externalization

antibodies.name: anti-vimentin surface staining antibody (LEGENDScreen / surfaceome panel)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: High-throughput LEGENDScreen panel; individual antibody clone and validation details not specified in source.

antibodies.name: anti-vimentin antibody (cell-based ELISA, senescent chondrocytes)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Used in intact-cell ELISA format without permeabilization; no clone or vendor specified.

expression_observations.context: Cell-based ELISA quantifying cell-surface vimentin (CSV) as membrane-associated marker on senescent human chondrocytes; intact cells, no permeabilization

Aptamer-mediated enzymatic cycling amplification combined with superwettable droplet microarray (SDM) chip enables single-cell-level detection of cell-surface vimentin (CSV) on intact tumor cells without permeabilization; CSV described as biomarker of aggressive/metastatic tumor cells

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Apoptotic primary human neutrophils (spontaneous or ATO-accelerated apoptosis)
accessibility_modulation.change: Vimentin undergoes caspase-mediated degradation and is externalized/displayed at the plasma membrane during apoptosis. ATO accelerates this surface exposure; GM-CSF delays it. Citrullinated vimentin surface levels are not significantly modulated.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface vimentin becomes accessible to extracellular binders specifically in apoptotic neutrophils, making it a state-gated target. Anti-vimentin therapeutics or imaging agents would selectively engage apoptotic cells, not resting neutrophils.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Chemically induced senescent primary human chondrocytes
accessibility_modulation.change: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) levels are upregulated and quantifiable by cell-based ELISA on the outer plasma membrane in the senescent state, establishing CSV as a surface marker of chondrocyte senescence.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Vimentin becomes accessible at the extracellular surface of senescent chondrocytes, enabling selective targeting of senescent cells with vimentin-directed binders without requiring cell permeabilization.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Non-tumor / normal cell context (baseline surface vimentin absent or low)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Multiple human cancer cell lines and hepatocellular carcinoma (Huh-7.5.1) cells
accessibility_modulation.change: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) is detectable by flow cytometry on intact, non-permeabilized cancer cell lines and confirmed by surface biotinylation/Western blot in Huh-7.5.1 hepatoma cells, indicating plasma membrane localization in tumor-derived cells.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CSV is accessible to extracellular antibodies (e.g., 7C11-D9) on multiple cancer cell types, supporting CSV as a broadly accessible tumor-associated surface target for antibody-based diagnostics or therapeutics.

structure.source: AlphaFold DB (whole-protein pLDDT — no ECD residues in topology)

surface_bind.attribution: © Marchand, Khakzad, Correia et al. — EPFL / Inria / Novo Nordisk

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: No evidence in the ledger defines a trafficking partner required for CSV surface presentation. Vimentin lacks a canonical signal peptide or transmembrane domain; its surface externalization mechanism (caspase cleavage in apoptosis, unconventional secretion in cancer/senescence) is only partially characterised. A co-receptor dependency cannot be ruled out.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: CSV is not uniformly distributed across the plasma membrane — its surface presentation is strongly linked to specific cell states (apoptosis, senescence, oncogenic transformation) and is absent or undetectable on resting/normal-state counterparts. No specific membrane subdomain (e.g., raft, apical) is defined in the ledger, but the state-gated nature of surface exposure functionally restricts accessible epitopes.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: Vimentin is a type III intermediate filament protein with no canonical extracellular domain, transmembrane segment, or signal peptide. Its surface presentation is non-conventional; the accessible epitope area and orientation on the outer leaflet are undefined and likely minimal compared to classical cell-surface receptors.

epitope_masking.rationale: CSV is presented in an atypical topology without a defined extracellular domain. Citrullination (an accessible post-translational modification on apoptotic neutrophils) and the conformational state of externalized vimentin filament fragments may mask or alter available epitopes. Antibody validation in the ledger is generally moderate or weak, consistent with epitope accessibility uncertainty.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry on live (non-permeabilized) cells using the novel anti-vimentin monoclonal antibody 7C11-D9 detected cell-surface expression of vimentin (cell-surface vimentin, CSV) across a series of cancer cell lines. This is a direct live-cell surface staining result confirming plasma-membrane accessibility of vimentin epitopes on intact cells.

spans.quote: The obtained data from flow cytometry analysis revealed that the 7C11-D9 antibody could detect the cell surface expression of vimentin in a series of cell lines, as illustrated in figure 3 .

evidence.claim: Anti-vimentin monoclonal antibody 7C11-D9 was validated by immunoassay with cross-species sequence alignment of the vimentin peptide target; a summary table of flow cytometry results for cell-surface vimentin (CSV) detection across multiple cell types is presented. This clip provides methodological detail for the 7C11-D9 antibody used in the companion flow cytometry surface-expression assay.

spans.quote:
Multiple sequence alignment results for the selected vimentin peptide in different species as well as the summary of immunoassay results for the produced anti-vimentin antibody (7C11-D9)
ND: Not done
Flow cytometry results of Cell-Surface Vimentin (CSV) detection in different cells.

evidence.claim: Cell surface biotinylation and isolation followed by anti-vimentin Western blotting verified the existence of cell-surface vimentin (CSV) in Huh-7.5.1 hepatocellular carcinoma cells. This is a direct biochemical confirmation of plasma-membrane-localized vimentin using sulfo-NHS biotinylation and streptavidin pulldown with WB readout.

spans.quote: A cell surface biotinylation and isolation experiment followed by anti‐vimentin Western blotting verified the existence of CSV in Huh‐7.5.1 cells ( Figure 6 A ).

evidence.claim: Cell surface protein biotinylation and isolation followed by Western blotting was used to measure the level of cell-surface vimentin in Huh-7.5.1 cells. This clip describes the methodological design: sulfo-NHS-biotinylation of intact live cells, streptavidin pulldown, and anti-vimentin WB — the paired method for the companion surface-expression result.

spans.quote: A) Cell surface protein biotinylation and isolation followed by Western blotting to test the level of cell surface vimentin.

evidence.claim: Vimentin (Vim), ENO1, citrullinated vimentin (cit-Vim), and citrullinated ENO1 (cit-ENO1) are expressed at the cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils. This directly establishes plasma-membrane surface accessibility of vimentin — including a citrullinated isoform — on primary human immune cells undergoing apoptosis.

spans.quote: However, Vim, ENO1, cit-Vim, and cit-ENO1 are expressed at the cell surface of apoptotic neutrophils.

evidence.claim: Prior work by the same group documented that vimentin (Vim) is degraded by caspases and expressed at the cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils. This review-level assertion from the abstract contextualizes a prior surface-expression finding and establishes a caspase-cleavage mechanism for cell-surface vimentin externalization.

spans.quote: We previously documented that Vim is degraded by caspases and expressed at the cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils.

evidence.claim: A cell-based ELISA protocol was developed to quantify cell-surface vimentin (CSV) as a membrane-associated marker of senescent chondrocytes. This establishes a functional-assay-class surface detection method (cell-based ELISA on intact, non-permeabilized cells) confirming accessibility of vimentin at the outer plasma membrane of senescent chondrocytes.

spans.quote: Here, we present a protocol to quantify cell-surface vimentin (CSV) as a membrane-associated marker of senescent chondrocytes using a cell-based ELISA.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) is established as a promising biomarker for detecting aggressive and metastatic tumor cells at the outer plasma membrane, requiring optimized analytical detection methods. This secondary assertion confirms the field's consensus that vimentin is surface-accessible on tumor cells and is a recognized target for surface-directed detection strategies.

spans.quote: Cell surface vimentin (CSV) has emerged as a promising biomarker for detecting aggressive and metastatic tumor cells, requiring optimized analytical methods for its detection.

evidence.claim: An aptamer-mediated enzymatic cycling amplification platform combined with a superwettable droplet microarray (SDM) chip enables sensitive detection of cell-surface vimentin (CSV) at the single-cell level. The use of aptamers targeting intact cells without permeabilization constitutes a direct surface-accessibility assay methodology for vimentin.

spans.quote: Herein, we report a platform that combines aptamer-mediated enzymatic cycling amplification with a superwettable droplet microarray (SDM) chip for sensitive CSV detection at the single-cell level.

evidence.claim: Surface staining was performed on intact cells prior to fixation with IC fixation buffer (Invitrogen) for 20 min at 4°C without permeabilization. This protocol detail (surface stain → post-fixation without permeabilizer) confirms the non-permeabilized, live-cell surface-staining paradigm used in the associated surface-expression assay, which is load-bearing for assay-context annotation.

spans.quote: Following surface staining, cells were fixed using an IC fixation buffer (Invitrogen) for 20 min at 4 °C in the dark.

evidence.claim: A high-throughput flow cytometry screening platform (LEGENDScreen kit with fluorescent barcoding) was used to profile cell surfaceomes. This describes a live-cell surface antigen screening methodology — the method family is flow cytometry / surfaceome panel, without permeabilization, applicable to discovery of surface markers including vimentin.

spans.quote: In this study, we adapted a previously introduced high-throughput flow cytometry screening platform for cell surfaceomes using a commercially available LEGENDScreen kit and fluorescent barcoding (Figs. 1 A and S1A) [ 25 ].

evidence.claim: Duplicate mention of the cell surface biotinylation and anti-vimentin WB result in Huh-7.5.1 cells (same finding as results_09). Included as a secondary corroborating anchor for the paired methodological clip; confirms same experimental result is independently stated twice in the source text, reinforcing confidence in the CSV finding.

spans.quote: A cell surface biotinylation and isolation experiment followed by anti‐vimentin Western blotting verified the existence of CSV in Huh‐7.5.1 cells ( Figure 6 A ).

evidence.claim: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) is detectable by flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells across a series of human cancer cell lines, confirming plasma membrane localization of vimentin protein in multiple tumor-derived cell types.

spans.quote: The obtained data from flow cytometry analysis revealed that the 7C11-D9 antibody could detect the cell surface expression of vimentin in a series of cell lines, as illustrated in figure 3 .

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry immunoassay results (anti-vimentin antibody 7C11-D9) confirm cell-surface vimentin (CSV) detection across multiple cell types, including different human cell lines; results tabulated per cell type indicating variable surface positivity.

spans.quote:
Multiple sequence alignment results for the selected vimentin peptide in different species as well as the summary of immunoassay results for the produced anti-vimentin antibody (7C11-D9)
ND: Not done
Flow cytometry results of Cell-Surface Vimentin (CSV) detection in different cells.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) is present at the plasma membrane of Huh-7.5.1 hepatoma cells, as confirmed by surface biotinylation followed by anti-vimentin Western blotting, demonstrating surface localization of vimentin in a hepatocellular carcinoma cell line context.

spans.quote: A cell surface biotinylation and isolation experiment followed by anti‐vimentin Western blotting verified the existence of CSV in Huh‐7.5.1 cells ( Figure 6 A ).

evidence.claim: Vimentin is expressed at the cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils; in the apoptotic cell state, vimentin undergoes caspase-mediated degradation and is externalized/displayed at the plasma membrane, representing a cell-state-induced surface presentation.

spans.quote: We previously documented that Vim is degraded by caspases and expressed at the cell surface of human apoptotic neutrophils.

evidence.claim: Vimentin (Vim), ENO1, citrullinated-Vim (cit-Vim), and citrullinated-ENO1 (cit-ENO1) are all expressed at the cell surface of apoptotic human neutrophils, establishing that apoptotic neutrophils display vimentin at the plasma membrane as a cell-state-associated accessibility event.

spans.quote: However, Vim, ENO1, cit-Vim, and cit-ENO1 are expressed at the cell surface of apoptotic neutrophils.

evidence.claim: Surface vimentin availability on apoptotic neutrophils is modulated by cell-state regulators: GM-CSF (antiapoptotic) delays vimentin degradation and surface exposure, while ATO (proapoptotic) accelerates it. This represents an accessibility modulation across apoptosis-inducing vs. survival-promoting states in primary human neutrophils. Citrullinated vimentin (cit-Vim) surface levels are not significantly modulated by either agent.

spans.quote: Although the degradation of Vim during SA was delayed by GM-CSF and accelerated by ATO, no significant degradation of cit-Vim, ENO1, and cit-ENO1 was observed.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface vimentin (CSV) is a membrane-associated marker of senescent chondrocytes; its surface levels are quantifiable by cell-based ELISA, supporting the concept that vimentin surface exposure is upregulated in the senescent cell state in primary chondrocytes. Senescence represents a cell-state-induced accessibility modulation.

spans.quote: Here, we present a protocol to quantify cell-surface vimentin (CSV) as a membrane-associated marker of senescent chondrocytes using a cell-based ELISA.

evidence.claim: Vimentin deficiency in the tumor microenvironment enhances CD8+ T cell-mediated antitumor immunity (increased CD8+ T cell proportion, upregulated cytotoxic genes Ifng, Gzmb, Tnf, Klrd1, activated ERK1/2 and NF-κB p65 signaling). This provides cell-type and functional context: vimentin is expressed in immune cells including CD8+ T cells and influences their activation state and effector function.

spans.quote: Functional assays demonstrated that Vim deficiency enhanced T cell-mediated antitumor immunity, evidenced by an increased proportion of CD8+ T cells, upregulation of cytotoxic genes (Ifng, Gzmb, Tnf, and Klrd1), and activation of inflammation-related signaling pathways, as indicated by enhanced phosphorylation of ERK1/2 and p65.

confidence_reasoning: A1 grades evidence as direct_multi_method across three independent assay families (live-cell flow cytometry, surface biotinylation/WB, cell-based ELISA) with zero contradicting entries. However, confidence is capped at moderate because: (1) state dependence is high — surface CSV is consistently linked to disease/stress states (cancer, apoptosis, senescence), not resting normal cells; (2) VIM lacks a canonical transmembrane domain, so surface accessibility reflects an unconventional, context-gated mechanism rather than constitutive surface expression; (3) antibody validation for several flow cytometry methods is weak (no KO controls, unspecified clones). These factors together reduce target-discovery confidence relative to a conventional surface receptor.
